danger_signals.visual_form: Yellow spike geometry on platforms (Level 4); death spikes scattered throughout levels
danger_signals.meaning: Instant damage or death zones; requires avoidance or specific input states (e.g., holding DOWN to pass through certain spikes)

danger_signals.visual_form: Walkers, ravens, and other hostile sprites populating the screen
danger_signals.meaning: Combat engagement required; defeat or avoidance necessary to progress

danger_signals.visual_form: Character sprite falls below onscreen height threshold (255 pixels from depth position); screen scrolls past character
danger_signals.meaning: Death condition; must jump or reach vehicle before falling too far out of bounds

danger_signals.visual_form: Speed-increasing challenge sections with projectiles, platforms, and hazards accelerating difficulty
danger_signals.meaning: Timing-based danger; reflexes and precision required

danger_signals.visual_form: Large distinctive enemy sprites with elevated complexity (Dark Queen, Rat boss with bomb arena)
danger_signals.meaning: Major threat requiring specific attack sequences and state monitoring

safety_signals.meaning: Health replenishment; restores HP (maximum of 6 HP available)

safety_signals.visual_form: Jet, surfboard, unicycle, rope, snake platforms appear as interactive level geometry
safety_signals.meaning: Safe transit mode; provides mobility advantage and often temporary invulnerability or damage boost

safety_signals.visual_form: Isometric-perspective platforms in side-scrolling levels; 2D solid floor in platforming sections
safety_signals.meaning: Safe standing space; collision detection with floor/ceiling prevents falling

safety_signals.visual_form: Unlisted interactive points in 4 designated levels
safety_signals.meaning: Progression skip; allows automatic advancement by two levels, indicating optional safe-path availability

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Three humanoid toad warriors (Rash, Zitz, Pimple); space-faring aesthetic
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Spacecraft Vulture; space-humanoid design consistent across all player characters

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Dark Queen tower/fortress; enemy sprite types (Walkers, ravens, rats, Intruder Excluder robots)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Diverse enemy roster indicating organized military/robotic force; boss-tier enemies (Rat with bomb arena) suggest command structure
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Dark Queen's planet; themed level architecture; fortress tower climb in final levels

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dramatic genre shifts: terrain (Vulture exterior → Turbo Tunnel → Arctic cavern → jungle/swamp → space terrain → underground tubes); background palette and scrolling patterns change per level
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy type and attack patterns shift; level geometry and obstacle types change; vehicle-based mechanics appear/disappear based on level design

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Enemy sprites animate attack frames; player sprite executes attack animations with enlarging body parts (Smash Hit variants show giant fists, horns, etc.)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player collision enables throw mechanics; defeated enemies disappear or drop legs/beaks as usable weapons; boss enemies follow scripted multi-stage patterns

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Vehicle-based traverse (Jet, Surfboard, Rope descent, Snake maze, Unicycle, Turbo Cable)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Player sprite transforms or changes position on vehicle geometry; sprite layers shift to show vehicle interaction; platform-based collision changes
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Input shifts to vehicle-specific controls; attacks become vehicle-restricted (kick on cable, shape-shift into wrecking ball); gravity and momentum rules change per vehicle type

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Sprite position calculation based on depth (0x493) minus midair height (0x475); visual indicator is character appearing low on screen
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Screen checks if onscreen height exceeds 255 pixels; if so, character dies; can jump from void if reaching exact 255-pixel threshold

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Boss encounter active (Dark Queen, Rat with bomb)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Large boss sprite with distinct animation frames; boss sprite may move around screen; bomb arena visible for Rat boss
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Boss has infinite or very high HP; specific attack sequences required (Rat must be hard-hit after bomb explosion, then finished with Boot attack); input manipulation can reduce animation time and control boss flight

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Two toad sprites on screen simultaneously; vehicle position may shift if one toad knocks other off (e.g., jet stays at position when knocked)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player 2 can grab/throw Player 1; collisions between players affect level layout (layout of Level 3 changes based on death timing with vehicle); scrolling behavior changes (Level 10 scrolls down if both toads exceed top of screen)

environmental_storytelling.example: Levels shift from action platforming (Vulture exterior, ice cavern) → vehicle racing (Turbo Tunnel) → rope climbing (descent to chasm) → underwater sections → snake maze → tower race descent, communicating world's hostile environmental diversity and testing player adaptability

Dark Queen's planet features fortress tower with flagpole weapons, robot-filled Intruder Excluder sections, and bomb arena with giant rat; architectural theme suggests technological/industrial antagonist faction contrasted with space-faring Battletoads aesthetic

Walkers (mechanical), ravens (organic), rats (plague-like), and Intruder Excluder (defense system) suggest a mixed-faction force on Dark Queen's planet; early levels feature simpler enemies, later levels escalate to boss-tier threats

Jet in Level 3, Surfboard in Level 5, Unicycle maze, Rope descent, and Snake-climb mechanics each reflect the specific terrain/environment; vehicle availability signals what world-state the player is entering
Obstacle sections increase speed as player progresses through, visually communicating escalating difficulty and danger; no faction identity, pure environmental pressure

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Charges attack while running in reverse direction; allows manipulation of attack angle and timing
Continues charging attack while airborne; landing continues running state, enabling mid-air attack setup

Plane-change effect varies by level: no effect on Levels 1,3,5,7,11; sprite disappears on Level 2; sprite drawing shifts vertically on Levels 4,6,8,9,12 (collision detection for floor/walls stays fixed, creating floating effect)
Bypasses collision on yellow spike (Level 4); ducking running (tap forward while holding DOWN) enables specialized movement

entity_behavioral_tells.state: Falling into pit at exact onscreen height of 255 pixels
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Can jump from void and escape death; falling speed and frame timing determines success

entity_behavioral_tells.state: Player touching vehicle while performing running jump upward
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Grants super high jump; vehicle acts as momentum amplifier (does not work with Level 7 airplane which is not gravity-based)

entity_behavioral_tells.state: Hit by walking toad in two-player mode
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Toad is knocked off; jet remains at position where hit occurred; allows players to reposition vehicle slowly

entity_behavioral_tells.state: Bomb exploded, then hard-hit by player attack
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Enters finish state; only killable with Boot attack after hard-hit following bomb explosion; otherwise has infinite HP; dying without vehicle triggers level restart vs. vehicle course restart
Input manipulation in first frame of attack can reduce animation time and prevent boss from flying around screen; direct input manipulation overrides character's own animation state

entity_behavioral_tells.state: Player grabbing side while snake bends
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Collision detection experiences major slowdown due to snake bending geometry creating overlapping collision zones; indicates physical strain on game engine

notes: Battletoads communicates world-state primarily through dramatic genre shifts and vehicle state changes rather than narrative. Danger is conveyed through hazard geometry (spikes, pits), enemy presence, and speed-based challenge escalation. Factions are minimally developed — the Battletoads are a heroic rescue unit, while Dark Queen's forces are a diverse hostile presence on her planet (mechanical, organic, and system-based enemies). The game relies heavily on visual contrast between safe platforms and hazard zones. Environmental storytelling emerges from level-to-level architectural theme changes. Player behavioral tells are mechanical rather than narrative — attack chains, running states, and vehicle interactions define gameplay, not character dialogue or cutscenes. The game's difficulty curve and obstacle acceleration serve as primary world-state pressure indicators.
